name: Апокалипсис
description: Apocalypse Sorcerers have seen the shape of things to come. They may have visions of the Great Beast ushering in the end of Etharis or dreams of Gormadraug waking and devouring the land. These prophecies besiege Apocalypse Sorcerers, plaguing and empowering in equal measure. Some find relief in putting these revelations into writing, producing texts that read as incoherent but slowly seep into the mind as sensible, even inevitable. When the apocalypse-bringer is freed to travel across Etharis unburdened, undoing the works of gods and men, magic will swell and pool, ripe for the taking. This sign of the end is also their gift.
shortDescription: Hasten or Stall the End of the World

features.name: Apocalyptic Spells
features.description: When you reach a Sorcerer level specified in the Apocalyptic Spells table, you thereafter always have the listed spells prepared.

features.name: Unhinged Asseverations
features.description: You are obsessed with documenting your visions. You gain proficiency with {@item Calligrapher's Supplies|XPHB} and you can create {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB|Spell Scrolls} in half the time and at half the cost in GP. In addition, when you create a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, you can ensorcell it. When you ensorcell a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, you must expend a spell slot equal to or greater than the spell's level and you can spend Sorcery Points to apply one of your Metamagic options. Any creature that knows at least one language can use your ensorcelled {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB}, which casts the spell with the benefit of the Metamagic option you chose. The {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} remains ensorcelled until it is used or you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Bear Witness
features.description:
You have prepared your entire life for the end of the world. While your Innate Sorcery feature is active, you gain the following benefits:

**Apocalyptic Inurement.** You have Resistance to Force damage.

**Recite Scripture.** Once per active Innate Sorcery, as a {@action Bonus Action|XPHB} you can use a {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} that has a spell with a casting time of {@action Magic|XPHB} action.

**Unflappable.** You are Immune to the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.

features.name: Arcane Apocrypha
features.description: Your obsessive reflections on the end of existence inspire your writing. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can create one {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} at no cost. It must be a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast. This {@item Spell Scroll|XPHB} disintegrates when you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}.

features.name: Forbidden Magic
features.description:
The end of Etharis is a time when magic is unbound and magical powers long hidden are unearthed. When you cast a Sorcerer spell using a spell slot, you can choose one option below.

{@b Excessive.} If the spell requires a Material component with a cost, you can cast the spell without the Material component. You take Force damage equal to four times the level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. This damage ignores Resistance and Immunity.

{@b Inexorable.} Taking damage can't break your {@status Concentration|XPHB} on the spell. When the spell ends, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.

{@b Pyrrhic.} If the spell requires an attack roll, the spell automatically hits and the attack roll is a Critical Hit. Your {@variantrule Hit Point|XPHB} maximum is reduced by an amount equal to four times the level of the spell slot immediately after you cast it. When you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, your Hit Point maximum returns to normal.
